mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that has stabilized or drifted down over the prior quarter signals reduced discount-rate pressure on equity valuations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in a lower cost of capital.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive-drift instances do not outperform baseline mean return with p>0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes risk-off equity repricing within the next 5 trading days, producing negative forward S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7 up from prior lows
failure_condition: In triggered instances, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC for the triggered subsample is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) over the same quarter indicates simultaneous weakening of consumer and housing-linked demand channels, which jointly forecast a broader consumption slowdown and depress S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days more than either series alone would predict.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 361046 and HOUST at 2120 in latest snapshot
failure_condition: The joint-decline signal's 60d forward return distribution is not significantly more negative than the single-series decline signals individually (interaction effect size test p>0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion regime (GDPC1 growing and UNRATE below 5%), a positive month-over-month change in INDPRO reflects strengthening industrial capacity utilization that supports near-term equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; the same signal is unreliable outside expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.2088, GDPC1 at 11248.3, UNRATE at 4.7
failure_condition: During periods classified as expansion regime, the correlation between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5d SPX return is not positive and significant (p>0.10), or the signal underperforms an unconditional INDPRO trend baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above a threshold associated with renewed price pressure while FEDFUNDS is already elevated, markets price in a higher probability of additional tightening, compressing forward equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean return (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating PAYEMS growth alongside a rising UNRATE indicates a compound labor-market turning point that is more predictive of an impending earnings recession than either series individually, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134966 thousand persons, UNRATE at 4.7
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-deceleration/UNRATE-rise signal fails to produce a Rank IC exceeding 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or its predictive power is statistically indistinguishable from UNRATE alone (interaction term insignificant, p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in HOUST (housing starts) reflects improving household confidence and construction-linked economic activity, which correlates with near-term positive equity sentiment and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST 3-month trend and forward 5d SPX return remains below 0.03 across any rolling 2-year window, or positive-trend instances underperform the unconditional baseline mean return (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflation-like regime where FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 4% while GDPC1 growth stalls (quarter-over-quarter growth near zero or negative), the combination of tight monetary policy and weak real output signals sustained equity headwinds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; outside this regime the same FEDFUNDS level is not predictive.
failure_condition: During periods meeting the joint trigger, 60d forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect between FEDFUNDS level and GDPC1 growth is not statistically significant (p>0.10) relative to either factor alone.
